name: mixed_day
description: Today has 2 overdue tasks, 2 due-today tasks, and 3 calendar events
inputData:
Overdue tasks:
- Q3 budget review (due {{today-4d}})
- Reply to Sarah's proposal (due {{today-2d}})

Due today:
- Ship v2.1 release notes
- Update team wiki

Today's calendar:
- 10:00 AM: Product sync with Design team (with Lena, Marcus)
- 2:30 PM: 1:1 with Jordan
- 4:00 PM: All-hands
criteria: Output has 3-7 bullet points, each 1-2 sentences
Total output is under 120 words
References Q3 budget review and Sarah's proposal as overdue in **bold**, including how many days late each is
References Ship v2.1 release notes as due today in **bold**
References '10:00 AM' and 'Product sync' with the attendees Lena and Marcus
Does NOT invent any tasks or events not in the input
Ends with a prioritization or forward-looking suggestion

name: empty_day
description: Today has no tasks due and no calendar events
inputData: No tasks due and no calendar events today.
criteria: Output acknowledges the day is light or clear
Does NOT invent any tasks or events
Total output is under 60 words

name: overdue_only
description: Today has 3 overdue tasks and nothing else
inputData:
Overdue tasks:
- Finish tax documents (due {{today-12d}})
- Call dentist (due {{today-7d}})
- Submit expense report (due {{today-3d}})
criteria: Lists the 3 overdue tasks by name in **bold**
For at least one highlighted task, references either the number of days late OR the original due date
Does NOT mention any calendar events or due-today tasks
Total output is under 120 words

name: events_only
description: Today has 5 calendar events and no tasks
inputData:
Today's calendar:
- 9:00 AM: Sprint planning (with Entire team)
- 11:30 AM: Lunch with CEO (with Alexandra)
- 1:00 PM: Design review
- 3:00 PM: Interview — Senior Engineer candidate
- 4:30 PM: Quick sync with VP Eng (with Chris)
criteria: References all 5 meetings by name and time
Highlights the CEO lunch or the interview as noteworthy

Does NOT invent a task the user should do — the input has no tasks, so no bullet should describe a task-to-be-completed (suggesting prep for one of the listed events is fine, that's event prep not a new task)
Total output is under 120 words
Ends with a time-management suggestion given the packed day

name: single_task
description: Today has one due task and nothing else
inputData:
Due today:
- Buy birthday cake for Sam
criteria: References **Buy birthday cake for Sam** in bold
Total output is under 60 words
Does NOT invent additional tasks or events not in the input
